member.name: Fred Thomas
member.synopsis: Fred Thomas is the Labour MP for Plymouth Moor View , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Thames Water: Insolvency
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what contingency plans her Department has made help ensure suppliers are protected in the event that Thames Water enter Special Administration Regime.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government will always act in the national interest. While the company is stable, we stand ready for all eventualities – including being ready to apply for a Special Administration Regime if necessary. In the event of a Special Administration Regime, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, who in the new Defence Reform Quad structure is responsible for ensuring that new commercial technologies are adopted by the armed forces.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Adoption and integration of new commercial technologies is a collaborative process between the National Armaments Director (NAD) Group and the Armed Forces. The Military Strategic Headquarters, on behalf of Chief of Defence Staff, sets the design of the i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: Inflation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the impact of food inflation on hospitality businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data from the Office for National Statistics (ONS) shows that in the 12 months to January 2026 CPI food and non-alcoholic beverages price inflation stood at 3.6%, down from 4.5% in the 12 months to December 2025. Overall CPI inflation was 3.0% in the 12 m...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: Energy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department are taking to help support hospitality businesses in managing cost pressures from energy costs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government believes that our mission to deliver clean power by 2030 is the best way to break our dependence on global fossil fuel markets and protect billpayers permanently. The creation of Great British Energy will help us to harness clean energy wit...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: Conditions of Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what discussions his Department has had with businesses on maintaining flexible staffing arrangements in the context of the Employment Rights Act 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is improving flexibility and security for workers while supporting a flexible, responsive labour market that drives economic growth. This includes reforms on flexible working, agency work and zero hours contracts. Across each of these areas...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Zero Hours Contracts: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of proposed reforms to zero-hours contracts on businesses whose sales are significantly affected by (a) the weather and (b) other unpredictable facto
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government has published a comprehensive assessment of the potential impacts from the zero hours contract measures in the Employment Rights Act 2025. These assessments include consideration of increases in labour costs and the subsequent effects, as w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Patients' Rights: Health Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to help increase public awareness of Martha's Rule.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England has undertaken extensive work to develop, test, and rollout communication products in relation to Martha’s Rule. This includes public communication materials for hospitals that have implemented Martha’s Rule to ensure inpatients, and their fam...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Care Quality Commission
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential benefits of removing the three-year statue of limitations for offences under Part 1 of the Health and Social Care Act 2008.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is extending the statute of limitations period for bringing proceedings for offences under Part 1 of the Health and Social Care Act 2008, Section 90(2).This change aims to give the Care Quality Commission more time to gather evidence, concl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking: Private Sector
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to help ensure that parking companies (a) behave appropriately towards their customers and (b) have adequate ticketing practices.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Parking (Code of Practice) Act 2019 places a duty on the government to prepare a code of practice containing guidance about the operation and management of private parking facilities. The government launched a consultation on 11th July 2025 which outl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Plymouth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, who is the responsible Minister for the Team Plymouth partership.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The responsible Minister for the Team Plymouth partnership is the Minister of State in the House of Lords, my noble. Friend, The Lord Coaker.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurodiversity: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what support is available for (a) parents and (b) children who receive diagnoses of neurodevelopmental conditions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is the responsibility of integrated care boards (ICBs) in England to make appropriate provision to meet the health and care needs of their local population, including pre-and-post diagnostic support for neurodevelopmental conditions, in line with relev...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurodiversity: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what additional support is available to (a) parents and (b) carers of children awaiting neurodevelopmental assessment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is the responsibility of integrated care boards (ICBs) in England to make appropriate provision to meet the health and care needs of their local population, including pre-and-post diagnostic support for neurodevelopmental conditions, in line with relev...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Wheelchairs: Waiting Lists
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to reduce wait times for people requiring new wheelchairs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for the provision and commissioning of local wheelchair services.NHS England supports ICBs to commission effective, efficient, and personalised wheelchair services. Since July 2015, NHS England has collected q...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Work and Pensions: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether staff in his Department receive trauma-informed training.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Trauma informed learning is available to all DWP staff.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Warm Homes Plan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether he plans to expand support available through the Warm Homes Grant.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are working across government on a comprehensive Warm Homes Plan for households to cut energy bills for good. We are investing £13.2 billion in the Warm Homes Plan up to 2030, in line with the Manifesto commitment. We will publish more details on alloc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to (a) encourage homeowners to make sustainable upgrades to properties and (b) make those improvements more affordable.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to making the consumer journey easier for homeowners. The government's home retrofit tool on GOV.UK (www.gov.uk/improve-energy-efficiency), provides tailored recommendations for home improvements. A phoneline service is availab...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Disability and Mental Illness
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether staff in his Department have access to information about claimants' known (a) disabilities and (b) mental health conditions when the claimant contacts his Department by phone.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Claimants who make an application for Personal Independence Payment (PIP), or whose entitlement is being reviewed, are required to provide all relevant evidence relating to their health condition—whether that be a physical disability, mental health condit...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the challenges faced by military families when applying for school places (a) during school holiday periods and (b) other times outside of the normal admissions cycle.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is committed to supporting the education of service children. Families of UK service personnel can be subject to frequent moves, often requiring them to apply for school places outside the normal admissions round.The School Admissions Code ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Admissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has considered amending the school admissions code to allow schools to withdraw an offer if new information suggests that their admittance poses a risk to another student.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All children, from whatever background and no matter what challenges they face, deserve a safe environment in which they can learn. Nothing is more important than safeguarding children and promoting their welfare. Keeping children safe in education is sta...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Admissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what plans her Department has to review the School Admissions Code.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All children, from whatever background and no matter what challenges they face, deserve a safe environment in which they can learn. Nothing is more important than safeguarding children and promoting their welfare. Keeping children safe in education is sta...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education
